bugs.description: issueCertificate supersedes an existing issued certificate during a normal issue call instead of rejecting unless the regenerate path explicitly opts in.

notes: Automated vitest passed. The required Playwright certificate contract command is blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirect loops; a diagnostic --no-deps run then fails because certificate fixture env vars such as EVENT_A_ID are absent. Typecheck fails with broad pre-existing repo errors outside the certificate issuance module. Manual code/spec review found a production behavior gap, but fixing it safely would require reconciling existing non-contract tests that currently assert the conflicting supersession behavior.

bugs.description: After a transaction failure retry was consumed, issueCertificate could throw the retry error or continue into an unintended third attempt instead of re-throwing the original transaction failure.

notes: Full Vitest passed after the fix (231 files, 4736 tests), including focused certificate issuance unit and mutation-kill suites (126 tests). Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this certificate issuance change. Manual verification confirmed the transaction retry state now stops after one retry and preserves the original transaction error on retry failure; persistence was not re-probed because the available contract browser fixture is blocked.

bugs.description: activateCertificateTemplate always issued the archive-sibling update even when no active sibling template existed, violating the no-sibling path that should only activate the target template.

notes: Full Vitest passed after the fix (231 files, 4736 tests), including focused certificate action and full-journey suites (114 tests). Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this certificate activation change, and a targeted typecheck grep found no certificate action errors. Manual review confirmed eventId-scoped target lookup, eventId-scoped sibling lookup, transactional archive-before-activate ordering when a sibling exists, and the corrected no-sibling activation-only path. Persistence was not re-probed because the available contract browser fixture is blocked.

Full Vitest passed (231 files, 4736 tests), and focused certificate issuance plus issue/regenerate route suites passed (4 files, 151 tests). Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this story; targeted typecheck output showed only a certificate-issuance test-file regex target error, with no production certificate issuance or route errors. Manual verification confirmed issueCertificate returns the inserted id, certificateNumber, and verificationToken; production issue/regenerate route consumers map those fields into their response bodies; the UI server-action caller ignores the return value. Persistence was not re-probed through the browser contract because the authenticated fixture is blocked.

Full Vitest passed (231 files, 4736 tests). Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this story. Independent Stryker dry-run with the locked stryker.config.json succeeded and ran 4578 tests without executing mutants. Manual verification accepted outcome b: .quality/state.json and qa/baselines/state-baseline-20260415T070514Z.json contain 72 scored modules, the committed snapshot has a plain-English partial-baseline note explaining the 30 unscored files and exit 143 timeout, no stryker.conf.mjs shadow config exists, and the travel-flow baselines are preserved at travel.ts=99.48, travel-cascade.ts=97.03, validations/travel.ts=100.

bugs.description: Archived events reached the certificate API auth catch-all and returned 403 instead of the contract-required 400 event archived response.
The single-issue certificate API ignored submitted body eventId mismatches instead of returning 400 and capturing the tenancy mismatch in Sentry.

notes: Full Vitest passed after the fix (231 files, 4736 tests), and the focused single-issue certificate route suite passed (15 tests). Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this story; the visible output remains in QA/test, Sentry, people, navigation, notification, and cascade files rather than the certificate API route. Manual review confirmed the route derives eventId from the URL, rejects body eventId/event_id mismatches via assertEventIdMatch with Sentry capture, maps archived-event auth failures to 400, keeps ops/read_only at 403, returns cross-event access through the sanitized 404 path, validates body and CME variables with Zod, and returns the inserted certificate id, certificate_number, verification_token, and issued status. Persistence was not re-probed through the browser contract because the authenticated fixture is blocked.

bugs.description: issueCertificate persisted caller-supplied renderedVariablesJson verbatim, allowing stale or empty master-person fields to become the immutable rendered_variables_json snapshot.

notes: Full Vitest passed after the fix (231 files, 4736 tests), and focused certificate issuance plus issue/regenerate route suites passed (4 files, 151 tests). Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this story; a targeted post-fix typecheck grep found no errors in certificate production paths. Manual review confirmed templateSnapshotJson is copied from the active template at insert time, renderedVariablesJson is now merged from authoritative people master fields before insert, and issuedCertificates update paths only touch status, supersession, revocation, download, verification, and sent-at fields, not templateSnapshotJson or renderedVariablesJson. Persistence was not re-probed through the browser contract because the authenticated fixture is blocked.
